sentence_reviews.text: Safety and policy alignment shape behavior with written principles, preference data, constraints, classifiers, refusal policies, and escalation rules.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF supports policies, controls, monitoring, and governance; RLHF/DPO papers support preference-data-based behavior shaping.
sentence_reviews.notes: Well supported as synthesis, but not an exact claim from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families), Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment is modeled as depending on Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families), Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization and feeding downstream nodes such as Conversational assistants & chatbots, Browser, desktop & computer-use agents, Autonomous vehicles, drones & navigation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
